steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.Pater: 'Pater' is a noun meaning 'Father' — here in the vocative
hints.caelum: 'Caelum' is a noun meaning 'heaven' in the accusative

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'pecco' — 'peccavi' has the perfect -vi ending
hints.pluperfect indicative: Pluperfect would be 'peccaveram' — 'peccavi' is simple perfect

hints.pecto, pectere, pexi, pexum: These are the parts of 'pectere' (to comb)
hints.pasco, pascere, pavi, pastum: These are the parts of 'pascere' (to feed/pasture)
hints.pango, pangere, pepigi, pactum: These are the parts of 'pangere' (to fix/agree)

hints.nominative: While 'pater' is the same form in nominative, here the son addresses his father directly — vocative
hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'patrem' — 'Pater' is vocative of address

steps.reveal: caelum = heaven (acc., after 'in' with motion implied)

hints.nominative: 'Caelum' could be nominative, but after 'in' meaning 'against' it is accusative
hints.ablative: Ablative after 'in' would mean 'in/within' — here 'in caelum' means 'against heaven' (accusative)
hints.genitive: Genitive would be 'caeli' — 'caelum' is accusative here

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'caeli' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'caeli' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'caeli' ends in -i

steps.prompt: What is the function of 'coram' in this sentence?
steps.target: preposition meaning 'before / in the presence of' (+ ablative)
choices: preposition meaning 'before / in the presence of' (+ ablative)
steps.reveal: coram te = before you / in your presence

hints.conjunction meaning 'when': 'When' is 'cum' — 'coram' is a preposition meaning 'in the presence of'
hints.adverb meaning 'together': 'Together' is 'simul' or 'una' — 'coram' means 'before/in the presence of'
hints.preposition meaning 'around' (+ accusati: 'Around' is 'circum' — 'coram' takes the ablative and means 'before'

translation: Father, I have sinned against heaven and before you
